tax: Placed at family rank, Choke Aeolians is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a settled foul-air body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a foul-air body with its own gas seep.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an invisible low pool that kills flame. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a guttering flame in still air — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Choke Aeolians occupies low still galleries and dips. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates a foul-gas pocket and recur with the foul-gas seep refills, sustaining themselves on heavy foul gas.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: pools low, follows and suffocates prey. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: foul-air body and motion-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: silent suffocation and downhill following. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Choke Aeolians matters because kills silently in low ground. It lets survey reports sort settled-air hazards from spinning ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Choke Aeolians is the family-rank grouping of settled foul-air elementals of the choke-damp kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a heavy foul-air body that pools low and suffocates silently, and divide below into genus groups by gas chemistry and pooling ground. The silent killer of the low galleries.
